units.title: All about me
units.subtitle: Meet your classmates and learn to say where you are from — aligned with Cambridge Prepare Lv1 Unit 1.
units.phonics_focus: Word stress in long words (nationalities like Brazilian, Japanese)

prepare_alignment.source: Cambridge Prepare 2nd Edition Level 1 — Unit 1 'All about me' (SB p14-17)

lessons.title: Hello, classroom
lessons.topic: Objects + people in the classroom. Determiners (singular).

steps.instruction: Look at the classroom. Who and what can you see?

questions.question_text: What do you put your books in?

items.definition: Something you carry your things in.
items.example_sentence: My bag is on the chair.

items.definition: A thing you talk and message with.
A picture you take with a camera.
items.example_sentence: Look at the photo of my family.

items.definition: A flat thing with legs you put things on.
items.example_sentence: The book is on the table.

items.definition: A small clock on your hand.
Someone you like and play with.
Someone who teaches you at school.

games.instruction: Match each picture with the right word.
Listen to the word and tap the right picture.
Look at the picture and type the word.

steps.title: Welcome to our class
steps.text: Hi! I'm Cassie. This is my class. Look at the photo. Here are my friends and my teacher. Nico is my friend. He is a student in our class. He has a phone and a camera. Sofia is my friend too. She has a bag and a watch. Our teacher is Mr Brown. He is kind. I have a book, a pen and a pencil on my table. I love my class. We are good friends!
steps.scene_description: A bright classroom with students sitting at tables, a teacher at the front, bags and books visible.

questions.question: Who is talking?
questions.locate_text: I'm Cassie. This is my class.

questions.question: What does Nico have?
questions.locate_text: He has a phone and a camera.

questions.question: Who is the teacher?
questions.locate_text: Our teacher is Mr Brown. He is kind.

questions.question: What does Cassie have on her table?
options: a book, a pen and a pencil
questions.correct_answer: a book, a pen and a pencil
questions.locate_text: I have a book, a pen and a pencil on my table.

questions.question: Is the teacher kind?
questions.locate_text: Here are my friends and my teacher.

steps.explanation: We use 'a' before words that start with a consonant sound (a bag, a pen). We use 'an' before words that start with a vowel sound (an apple, an orange, an egg). When we talk about ONE thing, we always use 'a' or 'an'.

steps.audio_text: TEACHER: Good morning! I'm Mr Brown. Welcome to our class. Cassie, are you here? CASSIE: Yes, I am! TEACHER: Nico? NICO: Yes! TEACHER: Great. Now, look at your tables. Open your books. Cassie, do you have a pen? CASSIE: Yes, I have a pen. TEACHER: Good. Sofia, do you have a pencil? SOFIA: Yes, I have a pencil and a book. TEACHER: Excellent. Let's start our lesson, class!
steps.scene_description: A teacher takes attendance and checks supplies in class.

questions.question: What does Mr Brown say at the start?

steps.prompt: What is in your bag today? Say 3 things using 'I have a...' or 'I have an...'.
steps.expected_text: I have a book, a pen and a phone in my bag.
prompts: Add two or three things: 'a book, a pen, a phone, a pencil...'
One person word: 'I'm a student' or 'My friend is a girl/boy.'

questions.question: Someone who teaches you is a ___.
True or false: 'a bag' and 'an bag' are both correct.

lessons.title: Where are you from?
lessons.topic: Countries + nationalities. be — singular (I am, you are, he/she/it is).

steps.instruction: Match the flag to the country.

items.definition: A big country in South America.
The biggest country in South America.
The biggest country in the world.
A country between Europe and Asia.

games.instruction: 60 seconds! Tap the right picture for each word.
Read the word and tap the right picture.
Flip the cards and find the pairs.

steps.title: Tell us about you
steps.text: Hi! My name is Alejandra. I'm 13. I'm from Argentina. I'm Argentinian. Hello! I'm Sang. I'm 12. I'm from China. I'm Chinese. Hi! My name is Lana. I'm from Russia. I'm Russian and I'm 11. Hello, I'm Sara. I'm 12 too. I'm from Brazil. I'm Brazilian. Hi! I'm Tariq. I'm 13. I'm from Turkey. I'm Turkish. Hello, my name is Martha. I'm 11. I'm from the USA. I'm American.
steps.scene_description: Six students from six countries introduce themselves on a class web page.

questions.question: Martha is from the USA. True or false?

steps.rule_pattern: be — singular (I am, you are, he/she/it is)
steps.explanation: We use 'be' to say what or where something is. With 'I' we say 'am'. With 'you' we say 'are'. With 'he', 'she' or 'it' we say 'is'. We often short these to 'I'm', 'you're', 'he's', 'she's', 'it's'.
examples: I am from Argentina. → I'm from Argentina.
She is from Brazil. → She's from Brazil.
It is a phone. → It's a phone.

items.sentence: I is from Argentina. → I'm from Argentina.
She am from Brazil. → She's from Brazil.
It am a phone. → It's a phone.
I am from Argentina. → I'm from Argentina.
She am from Brazil. → She's from Brazil.
items.corrected: She is from Brazil. → She's from Brazil.

items.sentence: It is a phone. → It's a phone.
It ___ a phone. → It's a phone.
I ___ from Argentina. → I'm from Argentina.
She ___ from Brazil. → She's from Brazil.

steps.audio_text: MARK: Hi! What's your name? ALEJANDRA: My name's Alejandra. MARK: Where are you from, Alejandra? ALEJANDRA: I'm from Argentina. I'm Argentinian. MARK: Nice! How old are you? ALEJANDRA: I'm 13. And you? What's your name? MARK: I'm Mark. I'm from the UK. I'm British. ALEJANDRA: Nice to meet you, Mark! MARK: You too, Alejandra!
steps.scene_description: Two students meet at school and introduce themselves.

steps.prompt: Introduce yourself in 3 short sentences using 'I'm...'.
steps.expected_text: I'm Linh. I'm 11. I'm from Vietnam. I'm Vietnamese.

lessons.title: Tell us about you
lessons.topic: Recycle objects + nationalities. be — plural (we/you/they are).

steps.instruction: Look at the team photo. Answer.

questions.question_text: How many people are in the photo?
What are the boys and girls? They are...

items.definition: A country in Europe shaped like a boot.
A country in East Asia, made of islands.
A country south of the USA.
The United Kingdom — England, Scotland, Wales, N. Ireland.
Someone who is in your class.

games.instruction: Listen to the word, then type it.
Listen and tap the right word.

steps.title: Our class web page
steps.text: Hello! We are Class 6B at City School. We are 24 students. There are 12 boys and 12 girls. We are friends. Our teacher's name is Ms Lee. She is from China. She is our English teacher. Look at our photo! Marco and Lin are from Italy. They are Italian. They have a camera and a book. Sara and Diego are from Brazil and Mexico. They are friends. Sara is Brazilian and Diego is Mexican. We are all classmates. We love our class!
steps.scene_description: A class of 24 students with their teacher posing for a school photo.

questions.question: What class are they?
questions.locate_text: We are Class 6B at City School.

questions.question: Who is the teacher?
questions.locate_text: Our teacher's name is Ms Lee. She is from China.

questions.question: Where are Marco and Lin from?
questions.locate_text: Marco and Lin are from Italy. They are Italian.

questions.question: Sara is...
questions.locate_text: Sara is Brazilian and Diego is Mexican.

steps.rule_pattern: be — plural (we are, you are, they are)
steps.explanation: When we talk about more than one, we use 'are'. 'We are' = me and other people. 'You are' (plural) = more than one 'you'. 'They are' = other people we talk about. We often short these to 'we're', 'you're', 'they're'.
examples: They are from Brazil. → They're from Brazil.

items.sentence: They am from Brazil. → They're from Brazil.

steps.audio_text: MS LEE: Good morning, Class 6B! CLASS: Good morning, Ms Lee! MS LEE: Today we have new classmates. Marco and Lin, please stand up. Hello! MARCO: Hello! I'm Marco. I'm from Italy. LIN: I'm Lin. I'm Italian too. MS LEE: Welcome! Class, are they our new friends? CLASS: Yes, they are! MS LEE: Great. Now, open your books on page 14. CASSIE: We're on page 14! MS LEE: Perfect, Cassie. Let's start.
steps.scene_description: A teacher introduces new students to the class on the first day.

questions.question: Who answers Ms Lee about the page?
Are Marco and Lin from the same country?

steps.prompt: Talk about you and your friends. Use 'we are' and 'they are'.
steps.expected_text: We are friends. We are Vietnamese. They are from Japan. They are Japanese.
prompts: 'We are' something together: 'We are classmates / friends / 11 years old.'

questions.question: Someone in your class is your ___.
True or false: 'They're' is short for 'they are'.

lessons.title: All about me — Review
lessons.topic: Recycle objects + people + countries + nationalities + be (sing/plural).

steps.instruction: Look at your friend's photo and answer.

questions.question_text: Where are you from? — 'I ___ from Spain.'

games.instruction: Climb the ladder! Choose the right word for each step.
90 seconds! Race through all the words you learned.

steps.title: Our class profile
steps.text: Hi! We are Class 6B at City School. Our teacher is Ms Lee. She's from China. We have 24 students from many countries. Alejandra and Diego are from Argentina and Mexico. Marco and Lin are Italian. Sang is from China. Lana is Russian. Sara is Brazilian. Tariq is from Turkey. Martha is American. Mark is British. We are all friends. We have phones, books and bags. We are happy classmates!
steps.scene_description: A class profile showing students from different countries.

questions.question: How many students are in Class 6B?
questions.locate_text: We have 24 students from many countries.

patterns: be — singular: I am, you are, he/she/it is
be — plural: we are, you are, they are

items.sentence: be — singular: I is you are, he/she/it is
items.corrected: be — singular: I am, you are, he/she/it is

items.sentence: be — plural: we are, you are, they are
be — singular: I ___ you are, he/she/it is
be — plural: we ___ you are, they are
be — singular: I ___ you are, he/she/it is
be — plural: we ___ you are, they are

steps.audio_text: REPORTER: Hi! I'm Joe. I work for the school magazine. What's your name? STUDENT 1: I'm Sang. I'm 12. I'm from China. I'm Chinese. REPORTER: Thanks, Sang. And you? STUDENT 2: My name is Sara. I'm 12 too. I'm from Brazil. I'm Brazilian. REPORTER: Lovely. One more, please. STUDENT 3: I'm Tariq. I'm 13. I'm from Turkey. I'm Turkish. REPORTER: Great. Thank you! You are amazing students in 6B. STUDENTS: Thank you!
steps.scene_description: A school magazine reporter interviews three students.

questions.question: How many students does Joe interview?

steps.prompt: Make a 30-second class profile. Say your name, age, country, and one classmate.
steps.expected_text: My name is Linh. I'm 11. I'm from Vietnam. I'm Vietnamese. My friend Sang is from China. He's Chinese.
prompts: Start with you: 'I'm [name]. I'm [age]. I'm from [country]. I'm [nationality].'
Add a classmate: 'My friend [name] is from [country]. He's/She's [nationality].'

questions.question: True or false: 'I'm British' means I'm from the UK.
